summary: Routine annual cardiology checkup. Dr. Johnson will review your recent ECG results and blood pressure readings.

chatHistory.text: Hello! I can see your latest results. Your blood pressure is slightly elevated. Let's discuss this in our call.
Thank you doctor, I've been a bit stressed lately.
That could be a factor. Please join the call when you're ready.

chatHistory.text: I'll be with you in about 5 minutes. Please stay on the call.

summary: Patient presented for routine general checkup. Blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, normal. Weight stable. No significant findings. Advised to maintain current diet and exercise routine. Follow-up in 6 months.

chatHistory.text: Your vitals all look good today. Keep up the healthy habits!

summary: Reviewed recent ECG results — normal sinus rhythm. Blood pressure slightly elevated at 135/88. Discussed lifestyle modifications including reduced sodium intake and 30 minutes of daily walking. Prescribed low-dose antihypertensive. Follow-up in 3 months with repeat blood pressure check.

chatHistory.text: I've reviewed your ECG. It looks mostly normal but I'd like to monitor your blood pressure closely.
Not at all. It's mild and very manageable with lifestyle changes. I'll send a prescription to your pharmacy.

summary: Patient reports recurring migraines 2-3 times per week for past month. Duration 4-6 hours each. Possible triggers identified: screen time, dehydration, irregular sleep. Recommended magnesium supplement, increased water intake. Prescribed sumatriptan for acute attacks. MRI not indicated at this time.

chatHistory.text: Based on what you've described, these are classic tension-type migraines. Let's work on identifying your triggers.
I think it gets worse when I use my laptop for long hours.
That's very common. Take regular breaks every 30 minutes and use blue light glasses in the evenings.

summary: Appointment was cancelled by patient due to scheduling conflict. No consultation took place.
